content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 107v–107r, LC PDF pages 211–212 | **Source Images:** DEU_014_page_001.jpg, DEU_014_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0152–pa_0153 — DEU_014_vaticanus_page_001.jpg, DEU_014_vaticanus_page_002.jpg

---

**1** Sons you are to the LORD your God; you shall not cut yourselves and you shall not make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.

**2** For a holy people you are to the LORD your God, and you the LORD has chosen to be for him a treasured people [am segullah] from all the peoples who are on the face of the ground.

**3** You shall not eat any abomination [to'evah].

**4** These are the animals that you may eat: the ox, the sheep of the flock, and the goat of the flock,

**5** the deer and the gazelle and the roebuck and the wild goat and the ibex and the oryx and the mountain sheep.

**6** And every animal that has a divided hoof, splitting the hoof into two hooves, [and] that brings up the cud among the animals—it you may eat.

**7** But this you shall not eat from those that bring up the cud or from those that have the divided hoof split: the camel and the hare and the rock-badger [shaphan], for they bring up the cud but do not have a divided hoof—they are unclean to you.

**8** And the pig, because it has a divided hoof but does not bring up the cud—it is unclean to you; of their flesh you shall not eat and their carcass you shall not touch.

**9** This you may eat of all that is in the waters: everything that has fins and scales you may eat.

**10** And everything that does not have fins and scales you shall not eat—it is unclean to you.

**11** Every clean bird you may eat.

**12** And these are the ones of which you shall not eat from them: the eagle and the ossifrage and the osprey,

**13** and the glede and the kite and the buzzard according to its kind,

**14** and every raven according to its kind,

**15** and the ostrich and the nighthawk and the sea-gull and the hawk according to its kind,

**16** the little owl and the great owl and the horned owl,

**17** and the pelican and the carrion vulture and the cormorant,

**18** and the stork and the heron according to its kind, and the hoopoe and the bat.

**19** And every swarming thing that flies—it is unclean to you; they shall not be eaten.

**20** Every clean flying creature you may eat.

**21** You shall not eat any carcass [nevelah]; to the sojourner [ger] who is in your gates you may give it, and he may eat it, or you may sell it to a foreigner [nokri]; for a holy people you are to the LORD your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.

**22** You shall surely tithe all the yield of your seed that comes forth from the field year by year.

**23** And you shall eat before the LORD your God, in the place which he will choose to make his name dwell there, the tithe of your grain, your new wine, and your fresh oil, and the firstlings of your herd and your flock—so that you may learn to fear the LORD your God always.

**24** And if the way is too long for you, so that you are not able to carry it, because the place which the LORD your God will choose to set his name there is far from you, for the LORD your God will bless you—

**25** then you shall exchange it for silver, and you shall bind up the silver in your hand and you shall go to the place which the LORD your God will choose.

**26** And you shall spend the silver for whatever your being desires: for the ox and for the sheep and for the wine and for strong drink [shekar] and for whatever your being asks of you; and you shall eat there before the LORD your God, and you shall rejoice, you and your household.

**27** And the Levite who is in your gates you shall not forsake, for he has no portion and inheritance with you.

**28** At the end of three years you shall bring out all the tithe of your yield in that year and you shall set [it] within your gates.

**29** And the Levite shall come—for he has no portion and inheritance with you—and the sojourner and the fatherless and the widow who are within your gates, and they shall eat and be satisfied; so that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hand that you do.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**v.1 — banim attem la-YHWH Eloheychem:** "Sons you are to the LORD your God." The declaration of Israel's sonship grounds the mourning prohibitions that follow. The body-cutting (gashing: lo titgodedu) and the shaving between the eyes are pagan mourning rites. LXX Vaticanus (p. 152) υἱοί ἐστε κυρίῳ τῷ θεῷ ὑμῶν — exact agreement.

**v.2 — am segullah:** "A treasured people." Segullah (סְגֻלָּה) denotes personal possession, a private treasury. LXX λαὸν ἅγιον — "holy people" — substitutes holiness language for the treasure metaphor, a meaningful interpretive shift.

**v.3 — to'evah:** "Abomination." The collective term precedes the specific lists. EMET renders it plainly without euphemism.

**vv.4-5 — the animal lists:** The ten permitted land animals. Five are common domesticated (ox, sheep, goat) plus five wild (deer, gazelle, roebuck, wild goat, ibex, oryx, mountain sheep — the LC lists seven wild animals, some of which are difficult to identify precisely). EMET follows LC names without speculative zoological precision.

**v.7 — shaphan:** "Rock-badger" — the Hebrew shaphan (שָׁפָן) is likely the hyrax (Procavia capensis). LXX χοιρογρύλλιον. Rendered "rock-badger" following traditional identification.

**v.8 — the pig:** The pig alone has the divided hoof but lacks cud-chewing. The LC reads veha-chazir ki-machseis parsah hu — the structure is chiastic: the pig divides the hoof but does not chew the cud, so is unclean.

**v.21 — lo tivashel gedi ba-chalev immo:** "You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk." The thrice-repeated prohibition (Exod 23:19; 34:26; here) is the basis of the rabbinic separation of meat and dairy. Here it comes at the close of the nevelah law — a structural juxtaposition. LXX οὐχ ἑψήσεις ἄρνα ἐν γάλακτι μητρὸς αὐτοῦ — "lamb" rather than "kid" (arnion vs. eriphos); minor variation.

**v.22 — aser ta'aser:** "You shall surely tithe" — cognate infinitive absolute construction intensifying the verbal command. LXX δεκάτῃ ἀποδεκατώσεις — exact equivalent.

**v.23 — ha-maqom asher yivchar YHWH:** "The place which he will choose" — the characteristic Deuteronomic formula for the central sanctuary. The verb yivchar is Qal imperfect, deliberate future/ongoing — the place has not yet been named.

**v.26 — shekar:** "Strong drink" — fermented grain beverage (distinct from wine). EMET renders literally as "strong drink" rather than modernizing to "beer." The LC is clear: la-yayin ve-la-shekar.

**v.28 — miqdey shalosh shanim:** "At the end of three years" — the triennial tithe for the poor. This passage establishes a secondary tithe cycle distinct from the annual tithe of vv.22-26. LXX (p. 153) ἀπὸ τριῶν ἐτῶν.

**v.29 — hager veha-yatom veha-almanah:** "The sojourner and the fatherless and the widow" — the Deuteronomic triad of the vulnerable, listed in consistent order throughout the book.

**LXX comparison (Vaticanus pp. 152–153):** The Vaticanus text of ch. 14 shows close correspondence to the LC/MT. The bird list (vv.12-18) exhibits minor orthographic differences in creature names. Most significant: v.2, where LXX renders segullah as ἅγιον ("holy") rather than rendering the treasure metaphor — a theological interpretive move. Page 153 picks up mid-chapter and continues through ch. 15.

— *EMET*
